title: Biometric Data Measures (PCP-derived, NOT in issued ADMP Guideline)
description: PCP No. 3/2025 §2.26-2.32 proposed 6 biometric-data-specific measures and a list of 7 biometric categories. The April 2026 issued ADMP Guideline does NOT contain these measures. Biometric data is referenced only in §8.1 as falling within sensitive personal data under s40. The detailed biometric measures are likely deferred to a separate forthcoming JPDP biometric guideline.

guidelineStatus.issuedReference: JPDP ADMP Guideline (April 2026) §8.1 — biometric data is sensitive PD under s40. No separate biometric-specific measures.
guidelineStatus.criticalNote: DO NOT cite the JPDP ADMP Guideline for the 6 measures below. They are PCP-derived best practice retained as analyst reference. Use s17 / s40 statutory obligations as the citation source for any biometric-data finding until JPDP issues a dedicated biometric guideline.

definitionCrossReference: automated-decisions/definitions.json — biometric data definition and examples

additionalMeasures.name: Transparency and control
additionalMeasures.rule: Inform data subjects, among others, how their biometric data is used and provide control over their biometric information.

additionalMeasures.name: Security and anti-spoofing
additionalMeasures.rule: Impose appropriate security measures and technical safeguards (encryption). To prevent identity spoofing (synthetic fingerprints, 3D face models), implement anti-spoofing measures such as liveness detection within the system.

additionalMeasures.name: Storage
additionalMeasures.rule: Encrypt biometric data during storage. Separate storage of original biometric data and derived features. Keep biometric (and other personal) information only as long as necessary for its purposes.

additionalMeasures.name: Accuracy
additionalMeasures.rule: Have appropriate processes in place to check the accuracy of personal information collected and created.

additionalMeasures.name: Disposal
additionalMeasures.rule: When biometric data is no longer required, ensure the corresponding entries are permanently deleted from the system.

additionalMeasures.name: Restricted secondary use
additionalMeasures.rule: Restrict secondary disclosures of biometric data for secondary purposes unless the secondary purpose is directly related to the primary purpose of collection and within reasonable expectations of the data subject.

internationalContextNote: Under the EU Digital Services Act, biometric data (as defined in EU GDPR) cannot be used for profiling. JPDP ADMP Guideline references this position but does not adopt an outright profiling ban for biometric data.
